title: Crispy Cured Venezuelan Date Empanadas
description: A crispy and savory Venezuelan dish featuring medjool dates and a shio-koji cure, perfect for a hangover cure, kosher friendly and cooked in individual mini cocottes.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the shio koji and water to create a cure.
Add the pitted medjool dates to the cure and let it sit for 30 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and salt.
Add the cold unsalted butter to the flour mixture and use a pastry blender to work it into a crumbly mixture.
Add the egg to the flour mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Use a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a cured date in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the date and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas in individual mini cocottes and brush the tops with the white wine.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
